title: Write an Executable Shell Script
description:
## Your First Automation Script

DevOps work is full of small scripts that glue tools together.

**Requirements:**
1. Create a script at **`/root/greet.sh`**
2. Make it **executable**
3. When run, it must print exactly: **`Hello, DevOps`**

> 💡 Start the file with a shebang (`#!/bin/bash`), `echo` the line, then `chmod +x` it. Run it with `/root/greet.sh` to test.

hints.title: Create the script
hints.body: Write a shebang and an echo. You can use a here-document to create the file in one go.

hints.title: Make it executable
hints.body: chmod +x adds the execute permission so you can run the file directly.

validation.description: Checks that /root/greet.sh is executable and prints the expected line.
